title: Global atmospheric methane concentrations
subtitle: Atmospheric methane (CH₄) concentration is measured in parts per billion (ppb).
caption:
Global atmospheric methane concentrations

Atmospheric methane (CH₄) concentration is measured in parts per billion (ppb).

This line chart tracks global atmospheric methane concentrations from 1983 through 2025, with the vertical axis ranging from 1,600 to 1,950 parts per billion (ppb). Two distinct lines represent the data: a blue line indicating the monthly average and an orange line representing the annual average. The blue monthly average line exhibits a consistent sawtooth pattern, oscillating up and down throughout the timeline, while the orange annual average line smooths these fluctuations to show the underlying trend. In July 1983, the chart begins with concentrations situated between 1,600 and 1,650 ppb. Both lines follow a steady upward trajectory across the decades. By the year 2009, marked on the horizontal axis, the values have risen to approximately 1,800 ppb. The rate of increase appears to accelerate in the later years of the timeline. Approaching the final data point in 2025, the blue line reaches peaks near the 1,950 ppb mark, while the orange annual average line sits slightly below it, exceeding 1,900 ppb. The gap between the lowest point in the early 1980s and the highest point in 2025 spans roughly 300 ppb. The visual progression shows no periods of sustained decline; rather, the concentration levels climb continuously from the bottom left to the top right of the chart area.
